006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonl;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
028
029/**
030 * Session object that lives for the duration of a single use of {@link JsonlParser}.
031 *
032 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
033 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe and is typically discarded after one use.
034 * </ul>
035 *
036 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
037 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonlSupport">JSONL Basics</a>
038 * </ul>
039 */
040@SuppressWarnings({
041   "unchecked", // Type erasure: elementType is ClassMeta<?>; toArray/convertToType return Object
042   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable
043   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
044   "resource"   // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's parser session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
045})
046public class JsonlParserSession extends JsonParserSession {
047
048   private static final String ARG_ctx = "ctx";
049
050   /**
051    * Builder class.
052    */
053   @SuppressWarnings({
054      "unused" // ctx passed to super(); parent uses it; Builder stores for constructor chain
055   })
056   public static class Builder extends JsonParserSession.Builder<Builder> {
057
058      private JsonlParser ctx;
059
060      /**
061       * Constructor
062       *
063       * @param ctx The context creating this session.
064       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
065       */
066      protected Builder(JsonlParser ctx) {
067         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
068         this.ctx = ctx;
069      }
070
071      @Override
072      public JsonlParserSession build() {
073         return new JsonlParserSession(this);
074      }
075   }
076
077   /**
078    * Creates a new builder for this object.
079    *
080    * @param ctx The context creating this session.
081    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
082    * @return A new builder.
083    */
084   public static Builder create(JsonlParser ctx) {
085      return new Builder(assertArgNotNull(ARG_ctx, ctx));
086   }
087
088   /**
089    * Constructor.
090    *
091    * @param builder The builder for this object.
092    */
093   protected JsonlParserSession(Builder builder) {
094      super(builder);
095   }
096
097   /**
098    * Opens a low-level pull-parser cursor over a JSONL document, bound to this live session.
099    *
100    * <p>
101    * Each top-level JSON value (one per line) is emitted as a flat sequence at depth 0; there
102    * is no virtual root container.  See {@link JsonlTokenReader} for the contract.  Same
103    * honored/ignored builder properties as {@link JsonParserSession#parseTokens(Object)}.
104    *
105    * @param input The input.  Accepts {@link Reader}, {@link CharSequence},
106    *    {@link InputStream}, <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>, or {@link File}.
107    * @return A new {@link JsonlTokenReader}.
108    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
109    */
110   @SuppressWarnings({
111      "java:S2095" // ParserPipe lifecycle is transferred to the returned JsonlTokenReader, which closes it via its own close(); the caller owns the cursor via try-with-resources.
112   })
113   @Override /* JsonParserSession */
114   public TokenReader parseTokens(Object input) throws IOException {
115      var pipe = new ParserPipe(
116         input,
117         isDebug(),
118         false /* strict */,
119         isAutoCloseStreams(),
120         isUnbuffered(),
121         getStreamCharset(),
122         getFileCharset());
123      return new JsonlTokenReader(pipe, new JsonTokenReader.Settings(isTrimStrings()), this);
124   }
125
126   /**
127    * Streaming array-element {@link RecordReader} for JSONL.
128    *
129    * <p>
130    * Unlike the inherited {@link JsonParserSession#parseArrayRecords(Object)} &mdash; which expects a
131    * bracketed top-level <c>[...]</c> array and is invalid for line-delimited JSONL &mdash; this aliases
132    * the JSONL line record stream: the {@link JsonlTokenReader} returned by {@link #parseTokens(Object)}
133    * is itself a {@link RecordReader} that yields one record per top-level line at depth 0.  Memory is
134    * O(1) in the number of lines.
135    *
136    * @param input The input.
137    * @return A new line-delimited {@link RecordReader}.
138    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
139    */
140   @Override /* ArrayRecordReadable */
141   public RecordReader parseArrayRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
142      return parseTokens(input);
143   }
144
145   @Override /* Overridden from JsonParserSession */
146   protected <T> T doParse(ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException, ExecutableException {
147      try (var r = pipe.getParserReader()) {
148         if (r == null)
149            return null;
150
151         if (type.isCollectionOrArray()) {
152            var elementType = type.getElementType();
153            var results = newGenericList();
154            var br = new BufferedReader(r);
155            String line;
156            while ((line = br.readLine()) != null) {
157               var trimmed = line.trim();
158               if (!trimmed.isEmpty()) {
159                  try (var linePipe = createPipe(trimmed)) {
160                     var item = super.doParse(linePipe, elementType);
161                     results.add(item);
162                  }
163               }
164            }
165            return type.isArray() ? (T) toArray(type, results) : (T) convertToType(results, type);
166         }
167
168         // Single object: parse just the first non-empty line
169         var br = new BufferedReader(r);
170         String line;
171         while ((line = br.readLine()) != null) {
172            var trimmed = line.trim();
173            if (!trimmed.isEmpty()) {
174               try (var linePipe = createPipe(trimmed)) {
175                  return super.doParse(linePipe, type);
176               }
177            }
178         }
179         return null;
180      }
181   }
182}
